summary: Iteration 30 - Bug fixes testing complete. Both reported bugs verified as FIXED: 1) Enterprise subscription checkout now correctly opens Stripe checkout when clicking 'Choisir ce forfait' or 'Souscrire' buttons, 2) Client dashboard 'Mon panier' tab is now visible and displays cart items from CartContext with quantity controls and checkout summary. All 17 backend tests passed.

issues: WebSocket connection fails on Render.com (expected behavior - uses HTTP polling fallback)

critical_code_review_comments: BUG FIX 1 VERIFIED: SubscriptionsSection.js handleSubscribe (line 29-44) now correctly calls subscriptionsAPI.createCheckout(planId) and opens Stripe checkout URL in new tab
BUG FIX 2 VERIFIED: ClientDashboard.js panier tab (lines 4056-4172) displays cart items from CartContext with enterprise grouping, quantity controls (+/-), remove button, and checkout summary
Backend /api/subscriptions/checkout endpoint (lines 1677-1721) correctly returns {url, session_id} with Stripe checkout URL
CartContext integration working - useCart hook provides items, removeItem, updateQuantity, getTotal, getItemsByEnterprise functions
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both bugs from this iteration are fixed and verified: 1) Enterprise subscription checkout - clicking 'Choisir ce forfait' or 'Souscrire' buttons in the Abonnements section now opens Stripe checkout in a new tab. The fix was in SubscriptionsSection.js where handleSubscribe was changed to call subscriptionsAPI.createCheckout(planId) instead of the non-existent subscriptionsAPI.checkout method. 2) Client dashboard cart section - 'Mon panier' tab is now visible in the sidebar under 'COMMANDES' section and displays cart items from CartContext with enterprise grouping, quantity controls, and checkout summary. The cart uses localStorage for persistence. WebSocket notifications fail on Render.com but HTTP polling fallback works correctly.
